title: What Lies in the Woods
author: Kate Alice Marshall
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "What Lies in the Woods" book by Kate Alice Marshall?
Options:
A. A darkened image of Liv, gazing upwards, her surroundings unclear, gave the impression she was rising out of a dark emptiness. Cass and Liv striding together, pinky fingers linked, Liv peering over her shoulder at the camera, at me, with a dazzling sliver of a smile. Followed by a photo of the three of us, united. I was visibly grasping the camera. We were seated on Liv's childhood mattress, shoulder to shoulder, and I was in the center. Cass and Liv were sticking out their tongues, looking at the camera. I was gazing at Liv, my eyes glistening. A small breath escaped my lips, my heart hurting.
B. An obscured shot of Liv, looking up, her environment vague, seemed like she was coming out of a black void. Cass and Liv walking side by side, smallest fingers hooked together, Liv glancing back at the camera, at me, with a bright slice of a grin. Then an image of the three of us, as one. I was clearly holding the camera. We were perched on Liv's childhood bed, shoulder to shoulder, and I was in the middle. Cass and Liv were poking out their tongues, peering at the camera. I was staring at Liv, my eyes shining. A little puff of air passed my lips, my heart aching.  
C. A shadowed shot of Liv, looking upward, her surroundings indistinct, seemed as if she were emerging from a blackened void. Cass and Liv walking side by side, pinkies hooked together, Liv looking over her shoulder at the camera, at me, with a brilliant slice of smile. Then a photo of the three of us, together. I was clearly holding the camera. We were sitting on Livs childhood bed, shoulder to shoulder, and I was in the middle. Cass and Liv were sticking out their tongues, looking at the camera. I was looking at Liv, my eyes shining. A little breath slipped between my lips, my heart aching.
D. A darkened photograph of Liv, gazing skyward, her surroundings indistinct, appeared as if she were rising from a dark emptiness. Cass and Liv pacing together, pinky fingers interlocked, Liv peering over her shoulder at the camera, at me, with a brilliant sliver of a smile. Followed by a photo of the three of us, united. I was visibly grasping the camera. We were seated on Liv's childhood mattress, shoulder to shoulder, and I was in the center. Cass and Liv were sticking out their tongues, gazing at the camera. I was looking at Liv, my eyes glistening. A small breath escaped my lips, my heart hurting.
Answer:

Cass and Liv striding together, pinky fingers linked, Liv peering over her shoulder at the camera, at me, with a dazzling sliver of a smile. Followed by a photo of the three of us, united. I was visibly grasping the camera. We were seated on Liv's childhood mattress, shoulder to shoulder, and I was in the center. Cass and Liv were sticking out their tongues, looking at the camera. I was gazing at Liv, my eyes glistening. A small breath escaped my lips, my heart hurting.
options.B: An obscured shot of Liv, looking up, her environment vague, seemed like she was coming out of a black void. Cass and Liv walking side by side, smallest fingers hooked together, Liv glancing back at the camera, at me, with a bright slice of a grin. Then an image of the three of us, as one. I was staring at Liv, my eyes shining. A little puff of air passed my lips, my heart aching.

I was looking at Liv, my eyes shining. A little breath slipped between my lips, my heart aching.
options.D: A darkened photograph of Liv, gazing skyward, her surroundings indistinct, appeared as if she were rising from a dark emptiness.
